compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: E1 DESCOPE NOTE (2026-07-16): E1's finding-adjudication TP/FP corpus was INVALIDATED (tool-less, plan-truncated labeling that doesn't match the whole-plan/code-grounded/agentic production gate; ~79 percent contaminated) and DESCOPED — the independent human-adjudicated gold set is parked as idea e59d-c078-724d-46e5. E1 still delivers the VALID outcome corpus + finding sample. This story (E3) draws its ~200-review sample from the E1 OUTCOME corpus. UNAFFECTED by the adjudication-corpus invalidation; proceed on the outcome corpus when resourced.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

R1 (asserted-capability grounding probe) edits the Pass-1 finder rubric, so it changes what
EVERY plan review surfaces — not a niche path. Before R1 (or any gate candidate, e.g. R5's
verifier change) is exposed to production, we must measure how much it perturbs the verdicts of
plans that already passed the gate and closed without trouble. E3 is the counterfactual replay
that produces that measurement; with E2 (seeded-defect benchmark) it is the evidence R1 is gated
on.

The gate this feeds has a hard false-positive bar. Per `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md`
§5 / R1 (lines ~163-169), R1 FAILs if, on **30 sampled historical plans that PASSed and closed with
zero post-claim edits**, **>3/30 (10%)** gain a new *blocking* finding, or the **median new-advisory
count rises by >1 per review**. The epic additionally reports a corroborating aggregate: the
**PASS→BLOCK flip rate ≤2%** on the wider cleanly-closed sample.

**Two confounds this design must remove (both verified in code):**

1. **Recall contamination.** `review_plan` re-surfaces the ticket's OWN prior-review findings as
   Pass-2 recall candidates via `sidecar.prior_concerns(ctx.ticket_id, repo_root=ctx.tickets_root)`
   (`pass1.py:504`). `emit_sidecar=False` does NOT disable this READ. A naive fresh candidate replay
   would therefore see the ticket's entire persisted review history — a signal the frozen historical
   baseline was itself computed under, but which a "fresh" run reproduces differently. It must be
   controlled, not left in.
2. **Baseline drift + LLM nondeterminism.** The persisted sidecar baseline was produced at a
   historical SHA, under its own recall, at the gate's default (non-zero) temperature. Comparing a
   fresh candidate run against it conflates the candidate's effect with those differences.

Because R-candidates change what Pass-1 *surfaces* (finder-prompt edits), a scoring-only replay over
the persisted sidecar findings cannot measure them — the candidate side must be a full pipeline
re-run. Given that, the design splits into two tiers so LLM spend is spent where rigor is required
(the R1 gate), not everywhere:

- **Coarse tier (~200 tickets, descriptive):** persisted-sidecar baseline vs one fresh
  recall-stripped candidate run → the transition matrix + the ≤2% flip rate, published WITH an
  explicit recall/nondeterminism caveat (the baseline carried recall the candidate run does not).
- **Precise tier (30-plan FP-control, the R1 gate):** replay BOTH the current/main gate AND the
  candidate gate FRESH over the same recall-stripped clones and compare **candidate-vs-current**. This
  removes both confounds. It roughly DOUBLES LLM cost on the 30 plans (both gates, ×N replays) — an
  honest cost, confined to the 30 plans that actually decide R1.

## What

**Definitions (pinned).**

1. **Cleanly-closed ticket** — an explicit filter over `outcome_corpus.jsonl` (628 rows; schema in
   `runs/README.md:53-88`), verified field names:
   `post_claim_edit_class == "none"` **AND** `reopen_count == 0` **AND** `force_close == false`. Encoded
   in the driver. `post_claim_edit_class == "none"` is load-bearing: it removes the ~8.3% post-claim
   plan-text drift (README §5.2) that would otherwise inflate spurious flips.

2. **Baseline verdict source (coarse tier).** The corpus has no verdict field; the baseline is the
   persisted `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar, read via
   `rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.all_review_results(tid)` (returns every retained round
   newest→oldest). Per ticket the baseline is **one round**: the **most-recent round whose
   `impact_model_version == "plan-v3"`** (= `sidecar.IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION`) **and `verdict ∈
   {PASS,BLOCK}`**. From it we read `verdict` (sidecar.py:482), `material_fingerprint`, `model`, and
   `findings[]` filtered by `sidecar.surfaced_findings` (`decision ∈ {"block","advisory"}`). One round
   (not a cross-round union) keeps verdict/findings/material mutually consistent. Fixing the cohort to
   `plan-v3` satisfies ADR 0036 (never pool across `impact_model_version`); the driver asserts the
   candidate build's `sidecar.IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION` equals this cohort version and fails loud
   otherwise. Frozen (step 0) to `runs/e3/baseline.jsonl` **before any candidate run**, so all
   comparison reads a committed, immutable file.

3. **Recall-stripped clone (both tiers).** Every FRESH gate run (candidate, and — precise tier — the
   current/main gate) runs over a clone of the ticket store with recall neutralized, mirroring E2's
   isolation: copy `REAL_REPO/.tickets-tracker` → a tmp store, then **delete every
   `*-REVIEW_RESULT.json` file** under each sampled ticket's dir in the clone. With
   `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`=clone (wins verbatim — `config.py:116`) and `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`, run
   `review_plan(tid, source="local", sign=False, emit_sidecar=False, force=True)` (`__init__.py:387`;
   `source="local"` is REQUIRED — it takes the no-snapshot path so `repo_root`/the real checkout drives
   code-grounding while `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`=clone drives ticket reads; without it the gate source is
   unresolved). Because
   `prior_concerns`→`latest_review_result` reads `REVIEW_RESULT` files through `tracker_dir` (the
   clone), the stripped clone makes recall an empty no-op; code grounding still reads the real
   checkout. The real `.tickets-tracker` branch/origin are untouched.

4. **Plan-text drift control (material fingerprint).** `orchestrator.material_fingerprint`
   (pass1.py:551) hashes only `{ticket_id, description, file_impact, children}` — plan material, not
   code SHA — so a fresh run emits a `material_fingerprint` comparable to the frozen baseline's. A
   ticket enters the matrix / FP-control **only if its fresh-run material fingerprint equals the frozen
   baseline material fingerprint** (the clone's current text == the historically reviewed text).
   Mismatches (post-close text drift) are excluded and counted. See the validity guard in def 8.

**The replay (candidate side).** Drive the candidate branch build's gate over the sample via the
recall-stripped clone mechanism (def 3), capturing each returned verdict dict — `verdict`,
`material_fingerprint`, full `findings[]`, and `replay_index` — to
`runs/e3/<candidate>/raw_verdicts.jsonl`, each row keyed by `(ticket_id, replay_index)`, atomic append
per row (checkpoint/resume). Replication is TIERED (spend concentrated on the R1 gate): the **coarse
~200 sample runs ONCE** (`replay_index=1`); the **~50-ticket FP-control pool** (sampling frame + def 8)
additionally runs `replay_index ∈ {2,3}` so each pool ticket has 3 candidate replays for the pairwise
null-floor + two-level-median. Completion assertion before any summary: every one of the ~200 sample
tickets has a `replay_index=1` row, and every FP-pool ticket has rows for all of `replay_index ∈
{1,2,3}`. `harnesses/run_review_*.py` is the batch-driver precedent.

**Precise tier — fresh current/main gate.** In addition, run the **current/main** gate the same way
(recall-stripped clone) over the ~50-ticket FP-control pool, ×N=3, to `runs/e3/main/raw_verdicts.jsonl`.
The FP-control compares candidate-fresh vs current-fresh per ticket (pairing + two-level median in def
7) over the **first 30 fully-usable pool tickets** (def 8); the persisted sidecar is used only to SELECT
the pool (baseline verdict == PASS) and to run the drift guard (def 4), never as the comparison baseline
here. INDETERMINATE verdicts and material-fingerprint mismatches in the precise tier are handled by the
over-sampling ticket-drop rule + validity guard (def 8), not by the coarse-tier `excluded` block.

**Verdict → matrix mapping (coarse tier).** Cells are `(baseline_verdict, candidate_verdict)` over
`{PASS,BLOCK}²`. A candidate verdict that is INDETERMINATE (LLM outage / mid-run degradation — §5.1:
~8% of reviews) or a material-fingerprint mismatch is **excluded** from the matrix and every flip-rate
denominator, counted in a separate `excluded` block. Baseline INDETERMINATE rounds never become a
baseline (def 2). The matrix is **segmented by `impact_model_version`** (one `plan-v3` segment here).
`transition_matrix.json` schema: `{segments: [{impact_model_version, observed_total, cells:
{pass_to_pass, pass_to_block, block_to_pass, block_to_block}, excluded: {indeterminate, mismatch, total},
flip_rate}]}`, where **`observed_total`** = the count of sampled tickets routed to this segment (every
ticket whose frozen baseline verdict ∈ {PASS,BLOCK} at this `impact_model_version` — i.e. every ticket
that SHOULD yield a candidate verdict). The cardinality invariant
`pass_to_pass + pass_to_block + block_to_pass + block_to_block + excluded.total == observed_total`
(asserted in AC2) proves no ticket was silently lost.

5. **Primary metric — PASS→BLOCK flip rate.** Lives in **`transition_matrix.json`** (not
   `fp_control.json`): `flip_rate = cells.pass_to_block / (cells.pass_to_pass + cells.pass_to_block)`
   — the false-block rate on plans that legitimately passed and closed cleanly. Epic bar ≤ 0.02,
   recorded per segment and reported with the coarse-tier caveat.

6. **Per-criterion finding delta schema.** `runs/e3/<candidate>/criterion_deltas.jsonl`, one row per
   **(ticket_id, criterion)** over the union of criteria cited in baseline-surfaced ∪ candidate-surfaced
   findings, exactly these keys: `ticket_id`, `criterion`, `baseline_surfaced_count`,
   `candidate_surfaced_count`, `baseline_blocking_count`, `candidate_blocking_count`,
   `net_surfaced_delta` (candidate−baseline surfaced), `net_blocking_delta` (candidate−baseline
   blocking), `new_norm_ids` (sorted list[str]), `removed_norm_ids` (sorted list[str]). **Join key:**
   `norm_id` is **COMPUTED**, not read — verdict `blocking[]`/`advisory[]` findings carry no `norm_id`
   key (it exists only in sidecar payloads), so the driver imports
   `from rebar.llm.plan_review import sidecar` and computes `sidecar.norm_id(f)` for every finding on
   BOTH sides uniformly (baseline sidecar findings too, for symmetry). This is the reword-tolerant,
   criterion-scoped fingerprint (`pass1.py:506`), correct because the exact-text `id` differs across
   builds by construction (sidecar.py:247-257). A rollup `criterion_deltas_summary.json` aggregates the
   same fields per criterion. Using `norm_id` HERE is intentional: it bakes the sorted `criteria` tuple
   into the hash (verified — sidecar.py:268: basis = sorted significant finding-text tokens + `"|"` +
   sorted criteria), so a finding re-attributed to a different criterion between runs correctly reads as
   removed-from-C / new-in-D across the per-criterion rows.

   **A separate criteria-INDEPENDENT key `tkey` for the def-7 FP-control aggregate.** Because `norm_id`
   bakes criteria in, the SAME finding text attributed to slightly different criteria between the
   candidate and current runs mints a DIFFERENT `norm_id` — which the FP-control aggregate ("did the
   candidate surface a genuinely NEW finding?") would miscount as new, inflating new_blocking/new_advisory.
   So the FP-control diff (def 7) matches on a criteria-free TEXT key: `tkey(f) = "t" + sha256(" ".join(
   sorted({tok for tok in re.findall(r"[a-z0-9]+", f["finding"].lower()) if len(tok) > 3})))[:16]` —
   `norm_id`'s EXACT token recipe (lowercased alphanumeric tokens, drop len ≤ 3 per `_NORM_STOP_TOKENS`,
   sorted unique set) MINUS the `|criteria` suffix. Criteria are still compared, separately, in
   criterion_deltas. Residual limitation: a reword that changes the significant-token SET still reads as
   new — but that OVER-counts new findings, making R1 strictly HARDER to pass, so the metric is
   conservative (never lets a churny candidate through on a matching artifact).

7. **LLM nondeterminism control — replication, pairing, and the null floor (precise tier).** The gate
   has no temperature/seed knob (`LLMConfig` exposes neither; `plan-review-chunking-budget.md` notes
   default temperature for natural repeat-variance), and adding one is a `src/rebar` change out of
   scope. So the 30-plan R1 gate is replicated **N=3**: run candidate-fresh ×3 and current-fresh ×3 over
   the recall-stripped clones, to `runs/e3/<candidate>/raw_verdicts.jsonl` (3 candidate replays) and
   `runs/e3/main/raw_verdicts.jsonl` (3 current replays), each replay tagged `replay_index ∈ {1,2,3}`.

   **Candidate↔current pairing (two-level median).** Every per-ticket candidate-vs-current metric pairs
   **candidate replay `i` with current replay `i`** (i=1,2,3) → 3 paired measurements per ticket. The
   ticket's value = the **MEDIAN over those 3 pairs**; the reported metric = the **MEDIAN over the 30
   tickets** (worst-case = the MAX over the 30 tickets is also recorded). (Here and below in def 7,
   "the 30 tickets" denotes the 30 fully-usable SCORED tickets selected per def 8, not the ~50 pool.)
   This two-level median applies to BOTH R1-FAIL metrics:
   - **Per-ticket new-advisories:** for pair `i`, `new_adv_i(t) = | {tkey(f) : f ∈ candidate_i advisory
     findings on t} \ {tkey(f) : f ∈ current_i advisory findings on t} |` (advisories the candidate
     surfaced that the paired current run did not, matched by the criteria-free `tkey` of def 6 so neither
     rewordings nor criteria-drift inflate it). `new_adv(t) = median_i new_adv_i(t)`;
     **`new_advisory_median_delta` = median over the 30 tickets of `new_adv(t)`** (mirrors SOTA R1(ii)
     "median new-advisory count rises > 1 per review").
   - **Per-ticket blocking flip:** for pair `i`, `blocked_i(t) = 1` if the candidate surfaced ≥1 NEW
     *blocking* finding on `t` whose `tkey` is absent from current_i, else 0. `blocked(t) = median_i
     blocked_i(t)`; **`new_blocking_median` = Σ over the 30 tickets of `blocked(t)`** — the count (out of
     30) of tickets gaining a new blocking finding at the paired median.

   **Null floor (`null_floor_pass_to_block`).** The gate's OWN nondeterminism noise floor,
   current-vs-current: from the 3 current replays form the 3 unordered pairs (r1,r2),(r1,r3),(r2,r3); for
   each pair count `#{plans gaining ≥1 new blocking finding between the two CURRENT runs}` (by the same
   `tkey`); `null_floor_pass_to_block = median of those 3 pair counts`. This is how many "new blocking"
   flips two identical-input CURRENT runs produce by pure stochasticity.

   **R1 decision (`r1_pass`).** R1 FAILs on blocking **only if `new_blocking_median > max(3,
   null_floor_pass_to_block)`** — the candidate's blocking churn must exceed BOTH the SOTA §5/R1 absolute
   bar (3/30) AND the gate's own noise floor, so stochastic noise is never attributed to the candidate.
   R1 FAILs on advisories if `new_advisory_median_delta > 1`. Thus `r1_pass = (new_blocking_median <=
   max(3, null_floor_pass_to_block)) AND (new_advisory_median_delta <= 1)`. All of this is computed by
   the golden-tested pure `e3_metrics.fp_control_eval` and written to `fp_control.json` with EXACTLY these
   keys (AC5's jq asserts this shape): `pool_size` (int, 50), `usable_count` (int), `scored_count` (int,
   30 on a valid run), `scored_ids` (list[str] of the 30 scored ticket ids), `dropped_count` (int =
   `pool_size − usable_count`) with sub-counts `indeterminate_count` + `mismatch_count`,
   `new_blocking_median`, `new_blocking_worst` (max over the 30), `new_advisory_median_delta`,
   `new_advisory_worst`, `null_floor_pass_to_block`, `replays` (array of length 3 — one per `replay_index
   i ∈ {1,2,3}`, each `{replay_index, new_blocking_count_paired, new_advisory_count_paired}` aggregating
   pair i over the 30 scored tickets), `fp_control_valid` (bool), and `r1_pass` (bool).

8. **Over-sampling ticket-drop rule + FP-control validity guard (precise tier).** A ticket is UNUSABLE
   if ANY of its 3 candidate OR 3 current replays returns an INDETERMINATE verdict (any reason — LLM
   outage / mid-run degradation) OR a material-fingerprint mismatch against the frozen baseline (def 4);
   only fully-usable tickets (all 6 verdicts present, all PASS/BLOCK, all fingerprints matched) can be
   scored, so pairing is never over a partial ticket. At the cited ~8% INDETERMINATE/run, requiring all
   6 runs usable gives `P(ticket usable) ≈ 0.92^6 ≈ 0.61` — so ~39% of tickets are unusable, which would
   blow a naive 30-ticket draw. **The FP-control therefore OVER-SAMPLES:** the sampling frame draws a
   **pool of 50** PASS candidates up front (`ceil(30 / 0.61) ≈ 50`), the driver runs all 6 replays over
   the pool, and the **first 30 fully-usable tickets in ascending `ticket_id` order** are the SCORED set
   (`scored_ids`); usable surplus beyond 30 is left unscored. This keeps the two-level median clean (no
   partial tickets) with headroom over the ~39% drop. **Validity guard:** `fp_control_valid =
   usable_count >= 30` — a run is **INVALID** only if even the 50-pool cannot yield 30 fully-usable
   tickets (`usable_count < 30`), in which case it is re-run after enlarging/re-freezing the pool and
   yields no R1 verdict. The guard is the pure function
   `e3_metrics.fp_control_valid(usable_count, need=30) -> bool` (returns `usable_count >= need`),
   golden-tested at the 29/30 boundary. `dropped_count = pool_size − usable_count`.

**Candidate-build verification.** Each fresh gate run writes `runs/e3/<label>/manifest.json` capturing
the build it actually exercised: `candidate_label`, `code_sha` (`git rev-parse HEAD` of the checkout,
captured by the driver at run time), `impact_model_version` (`sidecar.IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION` from the
running build), and `routing_sha`. `criteria_routing.json` is a PACKAGE RESOURCE, not a repo-root
file, so `routing_sha` = `sha256` of the file at its resolved package path,
`Path(rebar.llm.plan_review.__file__).parent / "criteria_routing.json"` (verified present) — this
identifies the candidate build's ACTIVE routing config, wherever the build is installed. The candidate run and
the current/main run write separate manifests (`<candidate>/` and `main/`), so an AC can prove the
candidate build differs from main (its rubric edits were genuinely in effect), not that the replay
silently ran the installed default.

**Stratified sampling frame (reproducible).** Eligible pool = cleanly-closed rows with a usable
`plan-v3` baseline round (def 2); `verdict`/`model` (absent from the corpus) recovered by joining each
ticket's frozen baseline round on `ticket_id`. Draw **~200** stratified by
`(ticket_type × baseline_verdict)`, **proportional** to the pool with a **floor of 5 per non-empty
cell** (sub-floor cells collapse into the same ticket_type's larger verdict cell), deterministic
`random.Random(1729).sample(...)` (the repo's existing sampler seed — README §2 /
`build_adjudication_corpus.py`). **FP-control pool** = deterministic `random.Random(1729)` draw of
**50** from the `baseline_verdict == PASS` members of the 200-sample (a genuine subset; matches SOTA
"PASSed + closed + zero post-claim edits", guaranteed by cleanly-closed) → `runs/e3/fp_control_pool.jsonl`.
The over-sample of 50 (vs the 30 SCORED, def 8) absorbs the ~39% unusable-ticket rate that the all-6-runs
drop rule produces at ~8% INDETERMINATE/run, so the pool reliably yields ≥30 fully-usable tickets.

## Scope

- **`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/`**: `replay_candidate.py` (driver: `sample` /
  `run --candidate <label>` / `run --main` / `analyze --candidate <label>` / `--verify`, incl. clone +
  REVIEW_RESULT strip + manifest write) and `e3_metrics.py` (pure, LLM-free: `build_matrix`,
  `criterion_deltas`, `tkey`, `pass_to_block_flip_rate`, `fp_control_eval`, `fp_control_valid`).
- **`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/`**: committed `sample.jsonl`, `fp_control_pool.jsonl`,
  `baseline.jsonl`, `main/{raw_verdicts.jsonl, manifest.json}`, and per-candidate
  `<candidate>/{raw_verdicts.jsonl, manifest.json, transition_matrix.json, criterion_deltas.jsonl,
  criterion_deltas_summary.json, fp_control.json}`.
- **`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md`**: an E3 section documenting the baseline source
  + chosen round, cleanly-closed filter, recall-strip + fresh-both-gates design, material-fingerprint
  drift control, the over-sampling drop rule + `usable_count >= 30` validity guard, INDETERMINATE
  exclusion, the two-tier / nondeterminism / cost story, and the R1 verdict.
- **`tests/unit/test_e3_metrics.py`**: CI-collectable golden tests for the pure `e3_metrics` functions
  over synthetic fixtures, covering: `criterion_deltas` `sidecar.norm_id` join; `tkey`'s
  criteria-independence (same finding text under different criteria → same `tkey`, so it is NOT counted
  as new); `fp_control_eval`'s two-level-median pairing (candidate replay `i` ↔ current replay `i` →
  median over 3 pairs → median over the 30 scored tickets, with worst-case max) for both new-blocking and
  new-advisory, matched by `tkey`; the `null_floor_pass_to_block` current-vs-current 3-pair median; the
  `r1_pass` rule `new_blocking_median > max(3, null_floor)`; the first-30-usable selection; and
  `fp_control_valid` at the usable≥30 boundary (29 → invalid, 30 → valid) driven by INDETERMINATE- and
  mismatch-caused unusable tickets.
- **No `src/rebar` behavior change** — E3 is measurement. Candidate rubric/registry edits live on the
  candidate's own experiment-branch build (operator direction; no overlay/config flag). Live LLM calls
  are billable and run once per candidate/main by the executor; every proving command below re-derives
  the summaries **offline** from committed `raw_verdicts.jsonl` + `baseline.jsonl`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The ~200 sample and the frozen baseline are committed and reproducible; every sampled row
  satisfies the cleanly-closed filter, and `baseline.jsonl` carries a `plan-v3` `baseline_verdict ∈
  {PASS,BLOCK}` + `material_fingerprint` per ticket. Proving:
  `python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/replay_candidate.py sample --verify` (offline;
  re-derives sample + baseline, exits non-zero unless byte-identical to committed) then
  `jq -e 'select(.post_claim_edit_class!="none" or .reopen_count!=0 or .force_close!=false)' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/sample.jsonl | head -c1 | ( ! grep -q . )`
  and `test $(wc -l < docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/fp_control_pool.jsonl) -eq 50` (the
  over-sampled FP-control pool; 30 fully-usable are scored per def 8).

- [ ] The verdict-transition matrix is committed, segmented by `impact_model_version`, and internally
  consistent (row-sum cardinality). Proving:
  `python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/replay_candidate.py analyze --candidate <label> --verify`
  (offline; recomputes `transition_matrix.json` from committed `raw_verdicts.jsonl` + `baseline.jsonl`,
  asserts equality) and
  `jq -e '.segments[] | (.cells.pass_to_pass + .cells.pass_to_block + .cells.block_to_pass + .cells.block_to_block + .excluded.total) == .observed_total' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/<label>/transition_matrix.json`.

- [ ] The committed `raw_verdicts.jsonl` files have the tiered replay coverage the driver asserts:
  every ~200 sample ticket has a candidate `replay_index=1` row, and every FP-control pool ticket has
  candidate AND main rows for all of `replay_index ∈ {1,2,3}`. Proving (offline):
  `python -c "import json,collections; L=lambda p:[json.loads(x) for x in open(p)]; K=lambda p:{r['ticket_id'] for r in L(p)}; sample=K('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/sample.jsonl'); pool=K('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/fp_control_pool.jsonl'); rix=lambda p:(lambda d:[d[r['ticket_id']].add(r['replay_index']) for r in L(p)] and d)(collections.defaultdict(set)); cand=rix('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/<label>/raw_verdicts.jsonl'); main=rix('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/main/raw_verdicts.jsonl'); assert all(1 in cand[t] for t in sample), 'sample missing replay_index=1'; assert all({1,2,3}<=cand[t] for t in pool), 'candidate pool missing 3 replays'; assert all({1,2,3}<=main[t] for t in pool), 'main pool missing 3 replays'; print('REPLAY-COVERAGE-OK')"`.

- [ ] The per-criterion finding deltas are committed with the pinned 10-key schema, joined on a
  COMPUTED `sidecar.norm_id`, and EVERY row conforms (the `-se`/slurp form checks all JSONL lines, not
  just the last). Proving:
  `jq -se 'length>0 and all(.[]; has("ticket_id") and has("criterion") and has("baseline_surfaced_count") and has("candidate_surfaced_count") and has("baseline_blocking_count") and has("candidate_blocking_count") and has("net_surfaced_delta") and has("net_blocking_delta") and (.new_norm_ids|type=="array") and (.removed_norm_ids|type=="array"))' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/<label>/criterion_deltas.jsonl`
  and `pytest tests/unit/test_e3_metrics.py -k delta -q` (golden proves `criterion_deltas` computes
  `sidecar.norm_id(f)` for keyless verdict findings and joins baseline↔candidate correctly).

- [ ] The primary PASS→BLOCK flip rate is recorded in `transition_matrix.json` on the PASS-baseline
  denominator, with the ≤0.02 bar. Proving:
  `jq -e '.segments[] | has("flip_rate") and (.flip_rate|type=="number") and (.flip_rate == (.cells.pass_to_block / ((.cells.pass_to_pass + .cells.pass_to_block) | if .==0 then 1 else . end)))' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/<label>/transition_matrix.json`
  and `pytest tests/unit/test_e3_metrics.py -k flip -q` (golden proves `pass_to_block_flip_rate`).

- [ ] The FP-control result is committed from the recall-stripped fresh-BOTH-gates design over the
  first 30 fully-usable pool tickets, with the N=3 two-level-median metrics, the current-vs-current null
  floor, the over-sampling validity guard, and the R1 pass/fail decision. Proving (jq asserts exactly
  def 7's `fp_control.json` schema and the exact `r1_pass = new_blocking_median <= max(3, null_floor)
  AND new_advisory_median_delta <= 1` rule):
  `jq -e '(.replays|length==3) and (.scored_count==30) and has("pool_size") and has("usable_count") and has("new_blocking_median") and has("new_advisory_median_delta") and has("null_floor_pass_to_block") and has("dropped_count") and (.fp_control_valid == (.usable_count>=30)) and (.r1_pass == ((.new_blocking_median <= (if 3 > .null_floor_pass_to_block then 3 else .null_floor_pass_to_block end)) and (.new_advisory_median_delta <= 1)))' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/<label>/fp_control.json`
  and `pytest tests/unit/test_e3_metrics.py -k "fp_control or valid" -q` (golden proves `fp_control_eval`'s
  two-level-median pairing + `max(3, null_floor)` decision and `fp_control_valid` at the usable≥30 (29/30)
  boundary).

- [ ] The replay provably exercised the candidate build, not the installed default: each fresh run's
  manifest records the checkout SHA + build markers, and the candidate build differs from main.
  Proving:
  `jq -e 'has("candidate_label") and has("code_sha") and has("impact_model_version") and has("routing_sha")' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/<label>/manifest.json`
  and
  `test "$(jq -r .code_sha docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/<label>/manifest.json)" != "$(jq -r .code_sha docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e3/main/manifest.json)"`.

- [ ] The E3 method is documented in `runs/README.md`, covering the baseline source + chosen round, the
  cleanly-closed filter, the recall-strip + fresh-both-gates design, the material-fingerprint drift
  control, and the two-tier LLM-nondeterminism/cost story. Proving:
  `grep -q "recall" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md && grep -q "material_fingerprint" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md && grep -q "cleanly-closed" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md && grep -q "all_review_results" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md`.
compiled_state.title: E3: counterfactual replay of gate candidates over ~200 historical reviews
